data.body:
Scope expansion (2026-04-26, from docs/proposed-mitigations-4-26.md, conflict resolution C3):

One mitigation absorbed into this epic's completion-verifier scope:

- **C3 → G3-M3 absorbed via coordination SC** — Add an explicit SC: 'The completion-verifier reads PRECONDITIONS events (delivered by epic 736d-b957) and refuses story/epic closure when unacknowledged degradation entries exist (decisions_log entries with degradation: true and no acknowledgment marker). This is in addition to the truths/artifacts/key_links verification that this epic delivers.'

Rationale: three concurrent expansions of the completion-verifier role were identified (truths/artifacts/key_links from this epic; epic-closure stage-boundary validator from 736d-b957; degradation-receipt check from G3-M3 mitigation). The coordination SC names 736d-b957 as the data source for stage-boundary signals while keeping 6068-cb2d as the home for verifier role evolution. Cleanly avoids two parallel verifier expansions.

Suggested SC framing: 'At story closure, the verifier checks: (a) task completion (existing), (b) truths verification (new), (c) key_links verification (new), (d) PRECONDITIONS-sourced degradation acknowledgment (new, reads epic 736d-b957 data). At epic closure, additionally: (e) story-level truths roll up to epic SCs, (f) no PRECONDITIONS degradation entries remain unacknowledged.'

This epic has no children yet (pre-manifest); decomposition will pick up the new SC naturally. Note: this epic relates_to 736d-b957 already; the dependency is structural — 6068-cb2d cannot deliver layer (d)/(f) until 736d-b957's writer/schema lands.
